Files: 34, Cohesion: 0.56, Coupling: 0.44

Description: The messaging middleware layer within the web viewer, providing fetch orchestration, message coalescing, call-rate limiting, request deduplication, polling dispatch, and view-specific data utilities consumed by the Preact UI hub.

<findings>

[observation] [warning] 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] Bidirectional imports with web-viewer (15 outbound + 12 inbound) suggest tight entanglement; the architectural model designates messaging as a subordinate layer to web-viewer, so any imports flowing from messaging back into viewer components should be audited for inversion.
[observation] [info] Polling utilities (batched-tick-dispatcher, tick-timer) and view constants (enrichment-thresholds, token-usage) are co-located with messaging middleware; separating polling infrastructure into its own sub-directory would improve cohesion and clarify the zone boundary.
[observation] [info] build.js and dev.js are build-config files that likely belong in the package root or a build-tools zone; their presence here may distort zone cohesion and coupling metrics.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "viewer-message-pipeline" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[pattern] [warning] use-facet-state.ts appears in the viewer-message-pipeline zone rather than web-dashboard-viewer, indicating the community detection algorithm is treating polling-adjacent hooks as messaging infrastructure. This artificially inflates the bidirectional cross-zone edge count between the two zones.
[relationship] [warning] Viewer hooks (use-project-status.ts, use-prd-data.ts, use-prd-websocket.ts, execution-panel.ts) import createFetchPipeline and createWSPipeline directly from messaging/index.js rather than through external.ts. external.ts only re-exports createRequestDedup, leaving the two most-used pipeline factories outside the gateway contract — a partial gateway that provides incomplete boundary enforcement.
[anti-pattern] [warning] build.js and dev.js are spawn-based orchestration scripts (esbuild, tsc, ESLint child processes) with no runtime viewer imports. Their presence in a runtime messaging zone inflates coupling metrics. These files should be excluded from zone classification via archetype pinning (config archetype) rather than left to distort cohesion signals.
[anti-pattern] [warning] tree-search.ts (packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/tree-search.ts) has zero messaging, WebSocket, or polling imports — it is a pure algorithmic utility classified here only because use-facet-state.ts imports SearchFacets from it, creating a transitive zone grouping artifact. It belongs in web-dashboard-viewer. Fixing the use-facet-state.ts zone pin removes both misclassifications.

</findings>

<insights>

- 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Moderate cohesion (0.56) alongside 15 outbound and 12 inbound cross-zone imports with web-viewer creates a near-circular dependency that risks merge into the hub zone over time.
- The zone spans genuinely distinct concerns — messaging middleware, polling timers, and view-level constants — which explains the subdued cohesion; splitting polling and view utilities from the core messaging pipeline would raise cohesion meaningfully.
- build.js and dev.js appearing in this zone suggests the community-detection algorithm is picking up build-config files alongside runtime middleware — these config files should be verified as not artificially inflating coupling metrics.
- Bidirectional imports with web-viewer (15 outbound + 12 inbound) suggest tight entanglement; the architectural model designates messaging as a subordinate layer to web-viewer, so any imports flowing from messaging back into viewer components should be audited for inversion.
- build.js and dev.js are build-config files that likely belong in the package root or a build-tools zone; their presence here may distort zone cohesion and coupling metrics.
- Polling utilities (batched-tick-dispatcher, tick-timer) and view constants (enrichment-thresholds, token-usage) are co-located with messaging middleware; separating polling infrastructure into its own sub-directory would improve cohesion and clarify the zone boundary.
- Zone "viewer-message-pipeline" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Viewer hooks (use-project-status.ts, use-prd-data.ts, use-prd-websocket.ts, execution-panel.ts) import createFetchPipeline and createWSPipeline directly from messaging/index.js, bypassing external.ts — external.ts only re-exports createRequestDedup from messaging, leaving the two pipeline factories outside the gateway contract
- The zone classification algorithm groups use-facet-state.ts (a viewer hook) alongside messaging middleware, suggesting the boundary detection treats polling-adjacent hooks as part of the messaging pipeline — this misclassification inflates the apparent bidirectional coupling count
- Viewer hooks (use-project-status.ts, use-prd-data.ts, use-prd-websocket.ts, execution-panel.ts) import createFetchPipeline and createWSPipeline directly from messaging/index.js rather than through external.ts. external.ts only re-exports createRequestDedup, leaving the two most-used pipeline factories outside the gateway contract — a partial gateway that provides incomplete boundary enforcement.
- use-facet-state.ts appears in the viewer-message-pipeline zone rather than web-dashboard-viewer, indicating the community detection algorithm is treating polling-adjacent hooks as messaging infrastructure. This artificially inflates the bidirectional cross-zone edge count between the two zones.
- tree-search.ts is a pure Preact algorithmic utility (case-insensitive tree search, facet matching) with zero messaging, WebSocket, or polling imports — it appears in this zone because use-facet-state.ts imports SearchFacets from it (line 17), causing the Louvain algorithm to propagate the zone assignment transitively. The root misclassification is use-facet-state.ts; tree-search.ts is a collateral victim.
- The import chain use-facet-state.ts → tree-search.ts means fixing the use-facet-state.ts zone pin automatically resolves the tree-search.ts misclassification without a separate archetype override — dissolving both into web-dashboard-viewer is the single fix.
- tree-search.ts (packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/tree-search.ts) has zero messaging, WebSocket, or polling imports — it is a pure algorithmic utility classified here only because use-facet-state.ts imports SearchFacets from it, creating a transitive zone grouping artifact. It belongs in web-dashboard-viewer. Fixing the use-facet-state.ts zone pin removes both misclassifications.
- build.js and dev.js are spawn-based orchestration scripts (esbuild, tsc, ESLint child processes) with no runtime viewer imports. Their presence in a runtime messaging zone inflates coupling metrics. These files should be excluded from zone classification via archetype pinning (config archetype) rather than left to distort cohesion signals.
- [call graph] 940 internal calls, 77 outgoing, 11 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
